points: New York is the epicenter of BESS restrictions.

As of the June 2026 tracker refresh, New York had over 100 active local BESS moratoriums — more than 80% of every active BESS moratorium in the U.S. Westchester remains among the most-restricted counties. Note: the Carina tracker now lists 5 active Westchester moratoriums after reclassifying several towns, but town records confirm Mamaroneck Village and Pound Ridge each extended their moratoriums in 2025–2026 — so the coalition tracks 7 active moratorium towns plus several outright bans.
Local control is decisive for standalone projects.

The State Office of Renewable Energy Siting (ORES) has no jurisdiction over standalone battery storage. A standalone BESS of any size must be permitted locally, so an active town moratorium or ban is binding with no state workaround. Only storage co-located with a solar/wind project ≥25 MW can use ORES to override local zoning.

Senate Bill S5506 (Kavanagh) and Assembly Bill A8378 (Levenberg) would expand ORES to standalone storage. Introduced Feb 2025, still in the Energy & Telecommunications Committee as of June 2026, not passed either chamber. Several Westchester municipalities (e.g., Yorktown) passed resolutions opposing it.
Most moratoriums are temporary — but some harden into bans.

Moratoriums are usually a pause while a town writes zoning; a large NY cluster expires mid-to-late 2026. In Westchester, Mount Pleasant and Mount Kisco have moved from moratorium to effective bans, while Port Chester adopted permanent regulations instead. The next several months are the highest-leverage window to shape the permanent rules.
links: NYSERDA — NY BESS Guidebook & Model Law

towns.name: Town of Cortlandt (Cortlandt Manor)
towns.subtitle: Northwestern Westchester — Hudson River to the Putnam County line
snapshot: LL 2-2024 (original) → LL 10-2024 → LL 2 of 2025 extended through Dec 31, 2025. Listed ACTIVE on the statewide tracker in 2026.
Significant developer interest; town wants time to consult experts and await State/County guidance before adopting siting rules.
HIGH — a committee is actively drafting the permanent local law.
summary: Cortlandt has had a moratorium in place continuously since 2024. After strong developer interest, the Town Board enacted LL 2-2024, convened a committee to draft a permanent law, and extended via LL 10-2024 and LL 2 of 2025 (through Dec 31, 2025). Still ACTIVE in 2026.
Because the town is openly drafting, this is one of the best chances to influence the actual ordinance — setbacks, fire/safety standards, permitted zones.
flags: Exact current expiration unconfirmed. LL 2-2025 ran through Dec 31, 2025 and the town is still ACTIVE in 2026, but a specific end date / final adopted ordinance could not be verified. Confirm via the Town Clerk / Legal Notices.
committees: Green Team / Climate Smart Task Force — sustainability body; natural ally.
Town Board BESS drafting committee — writing the permanent law (reach via Supervisor's office).
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — review future BESS special permits.
meetings: Schedule: Town Board meets monthly on a Tuesday at 7:00 PM, Vincent F. Nyberg General Meeting Room, Town Hall (1 Heady Street). Three Monday work sessions/month. Open to public.
Upcoming 2026 (7:00 PM): Jul 21, Aug 11, Sep 15, Oct 20, Nov 17, Dec 8.

officials.name: James F. Creighton, Cristin Jacoby, Robert E. Mayes, Joyce C. White

towns.name: Town of New Castle (Chappaqua / Millwood)
towns.subtitle: Central Westchester
snapshot: Moratorium adopted after a public hearing; applied to BESS and large-scale solar while the town develops regulations.
Supervisor Tipp cited NY's 70%-by-2030 / 100%-by-2040 goals alongside local protections after BESS fires elsewhere.
HIGH — town is actively weighing parameters to regulate BESS.
summary: New Castle paused battery-storage (and large-scale solar) development while it writes siting rules; listed ACTIVE on the tracker. Supervisor Victoria Tipp framed it as balancing NY's clean-energy targets against safety concerns from BESS fires elsewhere.
The town is explicitly weighing the parameters of a permanent regulation — real room to influence with balanced safety/siting standards rather than an outright ban.
flags: Confirm (1) the full Town Board roster + member emails — the site is JavaScript-driven, so only Tipp, Saland, Levin, and Morton were verifiable — and (2) the moratorium's adoption date and current expiration.
committees: Town Board — leads the moratorium and the eventual regulation.
Sustainability Advisory Board / climate committees — coordinate for a pro-clean-energy framing.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — future permitting authorities.
meetings: Schedule & agendas: Town Board meets at New Castle Town Hall, 200 South Greeley Avenue, Chappaqua. See the Town Board page for the current calendar, agendas, roster and emails.

officials.name: include Lauren Levin and Lori Morton (confirm full roster + emails)

towns.name: Town of Pound Ridge
towns.subtitle: Northeastern Westchester — broadest scope of the moratorium towns
snapshot: ALL battery storage, any size — one of only ~10 such jurisdictions in NY.
Six-month moratorium passed unanimously after a public hearing; extension hearing held March 24, 2026.
Concerns about fires, hazardous-material leaks and incidents nationally — adopted even though no BESS facilities exist in town.
summary: Pound Ridge's moratorium covers ALL battery storage regardless of size (only ~10 NY jurisdictions go this far). The Board passed the original six-month moratorium unanimously after a public hearing.

A March 24, 2026 hearing extended the moratorium, so it remains active. The town's strong sustainability infrastructure gives the workgroup credible allies to argue that well-sited, code-compliant storage can coexist with climate goals rather than being banned outright.
flags: Carina's tracker (refreshed June 26, 2026) reclassified Pound Ridge as LIFTED, but this appears to be aggregator lag: the Town Clerk noticed a public hearing on a 2nd extension of the BESS moratorium for March 24, 2026, so the ALL-BESS moratorium was extended and remains ACTIVE. Status kept ACTIVE; confirm the new expiration date via the Town Clerk / adopted local law.
Supervisor email listed as supervisor@townofpoundridge.gov to match the other members; the official page also references a .com variant. Both reach the office — confirm the preferred address.
committees: Sustainability Committee (formerly Energy Action Committee) — key ally.
Climate Smart Communities task force — ties BESS to state climate commitments.
Conservation Board & Comprehensive Plan Update Committee — siting and land-use policy.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — future permitting bodies.
meetings: Schedule: Town Board meets the 1st & 3rd Tuesday at 7:30 PM at the Town House (179 Westchester Avenue). In person, Zoom, or livestream; agendas post ~3 days ahead.
Upcoming 2026 (7:30 PM): Jul 7, Jul 21, Aug 4.
To get on the agenda: email the Supervisor's office ≥5 days ahead and copy the Town Clerk (townclerk@townofpoundridge.gov).

towns.name: Town of Bedford
towns.subtitle: Northern Westchester — Bedford Hills, Katonah, Bedford Village
snapshot: Moratorium adopted Aug 13, 2024; extended ~6 months — was set to expire July 16, 2025, extended through Jan 16, 2026 (confirm any further extension).
Concerns after fires at three NY BESS facilities since May 2023 and the volatility of lithium-ion batteries, especially large-scale.
HIGH — moratorium time is being used to study and draft rules.
summary: Bedford adopted a BESS moratorium on Aug 13, 2024 and extended it (originally to expire July 16, 2025) through January 16, 2026 while it studies safety and prepares regulations; listed ACTIVE on the tracker.
The moratorium supersedes the usual Town/Planning/Zoning Board application timelines. Engage during this drafting window.
flags: Bedford remains ACTIVE on the Carina tracker (refreshed June 14, 2026), so the moratorium was extended past Jan 16, 2026 — but the current expiration date is unconfirmed. The full Town Board roster / a direct phone weren't verified. Confirm via the Town Board page and Agenda Center.
committees: Town Board — adopted and extends the moratorium.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — future permitting.
Conservation Board / sustainability committees — likely allies (confirm names).
meetings: Schedule: Town Board meets the 1st & 3rd Tuesdays at the Town House (321 Bedford Road, Bedford Hills); public open sessions at the start and end; Zoom available.

officials.name: 5-member board incl. Bobbi Bittker and Andres L. Castillo (confirm full roster)

towns.name: City of Peekskill
towns.subtitle: Northwestern Westchester — Hudson River city
snapshot: Moratorium on lithium-ion BESS over 80 kW enacted May 2025 (unanimous); extended in late 2025.
City Attorney cited the volatile nature of lithium-ion batteries and the need for fire-suppression and ground/water-impact standards.
HIGH — city is studying and drafting BESS regulations.
summary: Peekskill enacted a moratorium on lithium-ion BESS over 80 kW in May 2025 (passed unanimously after a public hearing) and extended it in late 2025 while it studies safety and appropriate standards.
As a city with a Common Council, Peekskill's process differs from the towns — engagement runs through the Mayor and Council.
flags: Confirm the current expiration date and the six Common Council members' names/contacts on the city site.
committees: Mayor & Common Council — the legislative body for any BESS law.
Planning Commission / Zoning Board of Appeals — permitting bodies.
meetings: Schedule: Common Council meets Mondays — a Committee of the Whole work session followed by the Common Council meeting (evening sessions typically 7:00 PM work session / 7:30 PM meeting).

towns.name: Village of Mamaroneck
towns.subtitle: Southeastern Westchester — Long Island Sound shoreline
snapshot: Moratorium extended multiple times in 2025 (LL 7-2025, LL 14-2025) amending Chapter 342 (Zoning) while permanent regulations are prepared.
Standard safety/zoning study period; the Village Attorney presented proposals at an Oct 14, 2025 work session.
summary: The Village of Mamaroneck has repeatedly extended its BESS moratorium through 2025 (Local Laws 7-2025 and 14-2025, amending Chapter 342, Zoning) while preparing permanent regulations.
With the Village Attorney actively presenting BESS proposals, there is an open window to weigh in on the permanent standards.
flags: Carina's tracker (refreshed June 26, 2026) reclassified Mamaroneck Village as LIFTED, but Village records contradict this: the moratorium was extended repeatedly in 2025, most recently by Local Law 14-2025 amending Chapter 342 (Zoning). Status kept ACTIVE; confirm the current LL 14-2025 expiration date on the Village site.
Confirm the current moratorium expiration and the Mayor/Trustee roster + contacts on the Village site.
committees: Board of Trustees — Mayor + 4 Trustees; the legislative body.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — permitting.
meetings: Schedule: Board of Trustees meets the 2nd & 4th Mondays (work session 5:00 PM, regular meeting 8:00 PM) at the Village Courtroom, 169 Mt. Pleasant Avenue, Mamaroneck, NY 10543.

officials.name: Mayor + four Trustees (confirm current names/emails via the Trustees page)

towns.name: City of Yonkers
towns.subtitle: Southwestern Westchester — the county's largest city
snapshot: City Council adopted a 180-day moratorium (extendable another 180 days) on lithium-ion battery (and self-storage) facilities; extended in 2025.
Mayor Spano cited the need to study environmental, neighborhood and public-health impacts under SEQRA; a consultant was hired to draft regulations.
summary: At Mayor Mike Spano's request, the Yonkers City Council adopted a moratorium on lithium-ion battery facilities and extended it in 2025 while a hired consultant studies BESS regulations under SEQRA.
No BESS facilities currently operate in the city; a proposal at 1100 Saw Mill River Road is under review. Engagement runs through the Mayor and City Council.
flags: Confirm the current expiration and the City Council roster/contacts on the city site.
committees: City Council — Council President + council members; the legislative body.
Planning Board / Zoning Board of Appeals — permitting.
meetings: Schedule: City Council meetings are held at City Hall, 40 South Broadway. Check the city site for the current schedule and agendas.

officials.name: Deana R. Norman, Corazon Pineda Isaac, John Rubbo, Mike Breen, Tim Hodges

towns.name: Town of Greenburgh
towns.subtitle: Central Westchester — six villages + unincorporated area
snapshot: Already has a BESS law; the Town is considering amendments as part of broader land-use code changes.
Amendments were referred to the Planning Board for review and recommendation over several months — an open process to influence.
summary: Unlike the moratorium towns, Greenburgh already has a BESS law and is now revising it as part of a broader package of land-use code amendments. The Town Board referred the changes to the Planning Board, which will hear staff presentations and discuss them over several months.
Because the rules are being actively reworked, the workgroup can engage on the substance of the amendments at Town Board and Planning Board meetings.
flags: The specifics of the existing BESS law and the proposed amendments should be reviewed directly; this town was added as a relevant non-moratorium jurisdiction. Confirm the Town Board roster/contacts.
committees: Town Board — 4 Council members + Supervisor; initiates code amendments.
Planning Board — currently reviewing the BESS amendments.
Greenburgh Nature Center / sustainability bodies — potential allies.
meetings: Schedule: Town Board meets the 2nd & 4th Wednesday at 7:30 PM at Greenburgh Town Hall (177 Hillside Avenue); in person or Zoom.

towns.name: Village of Rye Brook
towns.subtitle: Southeastern Westchester — the clearest “get ahead of it” opportunity
snapshot: No BESS moratorium or dedicated local law identified; not in the statewide tracker as of May 2026. NY's 2025 Fire Code applies as a baseline.
No reactive ban yet — engaging early lets the workgroup help craft a sensible framework before a project triggers a hasty moratorium.
summary: Rye Brook has no BESS moratorium or ordinance on record and does not appear in the statewide tracker as of May 2026. NY's 2025 Fire Code and the State BESS Model Law are the baseline.
Best opportunity to get ahead of the issue: approach the Board of Trustees, Planning Board, and the Village's Sustainability Committee now to encourage a balanced, safety-forward ordinance.
flags: Individual trustee emails are not published; reach officials through the Village Administrator's office. “No moratorium” is an absence-of-record finding as of May 2026 — confirm nothing has been introduced since.
committees: Board of Trustees — the legislative body; any BESS law starts here.
Sustainability Committee — existing ally for a clean-energy-friendly approach.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — would review BESS site plans/variances.
meetings: Schedule: Board of Trustees meets the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of every month at Village Hall (938 King Street).

officials.name: Susan R. Epstein, David M. Heiser, Donald T. Krom, Jr., Salvatore W. Morlino

towns.name: Town / Village of Harrison
towns.subtitle: Central Westchester — coterminous Town and Village government
snapshot: LL 1 of 2025 — a 6-month moratorium effective March 6, 2025; lapsed ~September 2025 (Town Code Article XVII).
Town Board, Planning Board and ZBA wanted more on fire suppression, emergency response, gas emissions, groundwater, and siting standards.
MEDIUM — no active pause; watch for a permanent BESS code and weigh in before it is finalized.
summary: Harrison adopted a six-month moratorium (LL 1-2025) effective March 6, 2025. It expired ~6 months later absent a Town Board extension, and the statewide tracker now lists Harrison as EXPIRED — no active pause on BESS applications.
Confirm whether Harrison intends a permanent BESS ordinance and offer input on safety/siting standards before any new law is locked in.
flags: Councilmember emails follow the town's standard format (firstinitiallastname@harrison-ny.gov) from a directory listing; not each confirmed on the official board page. Verify before formal outreach.
committees: Town / Village Board — adopted, and would extend or replace, the moratorium.
Planning Board — reviews site plans/special permits; cited in the rationale.
Zoning Board of Appeals — also cited as needing more BESS information.
meetings: Schedule: Town/Village Board meets twice monthly — typically 1st & 3rd Thursday at 7:00 PM (Sep–May) — and once monthly (1st Thursday, 7:00 PM) in Feb, Jul, Aug.
Location: Alfred F. Sulla, Jr. Municipal Building, 1 Heineman Place, Harrison, NY 10528.

towns.name: Town of Yorktown
towns.subtitle: Northern Westchester — Yorktown Heights, Jefferson Valley
snapshot: Public hearing held Oct 1, 2024 on a BESS moratorium; the moratorium is listed EXPIRED on the statewide tracker.
The Town Board passed a resolution opposing state bills S5506 / A8378 that would let ORES preempt local BESS siting.
MEDIUM — no active pause; watch for permanent regulations and the town's state-preemption stance.
summary: Yorktown enacted a BESS moratorium following an Oct 1, 2024 public hearing; it is now listed EXPIRED. The Town Board has also formally opposed S5506/A8378, signaling it wants to keep BESS siting under local control.
Confirm whether permanent regulations are in progress and engage on standards as well as the town's state-preemption advocacy.
flags: Exact moratorium dates and any permanent-ordinance status should be confirmed via the Town Clerk / eCode360.
committees: Town Board — the legislative body; passed the moratorium and the anti-preemption resolution.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — permitting.
meetings: Schedule: Town Board regular meetings are usually the 1st & 3rd Tuesdays in the Town Hall Board Room (work sessions on additional Tuesdays). Televised on Channel 20 / YGTV live feed.

officials.name: Sergio Esposito (Deputy Supervisor), Luciana Haughwout, Patrick Murphy, Susan Siegel

towns.name: Village of Port Chester
towns.subtitle: Southeastern Westchester — Long Island Sound, NY/CT border
snapshot: Ended its moratorium in early 2025 and adopted permanent BESS regulations amending Chapter 345 (Zoning), after a SEQRA negative declaration.
Prompted partly by a BESS application on North Main Street; the Village chose to regulate rather than ban or keep pausing.
MODERATE — rules exist; engage on amendments or specific applications.
summary: Port Chester moved from a temporary moratorium to permanent BESS zoning regulations in early 2025 (amending Chapter 345), adopting a SEQRA negative declaration in the process. The trigger was a pending BESS application on North Main Street.
Because Port Chester now allows BESS under defined standards, it is a useful reference model — and a place to engage on how those standards are applied or amended.
flags: The Mayor's full name and the specifics of the adopted BESS standards should be confirmed from the adopted local law / Chapter 345.
committees: Board of Trustees — Mayor + Trustees; adopted the regulations.
Planning Commission / Zoning Board of Appeals — apply the BESS standards.
meetings: Schedule & agendas: Board of Trustees agendas are posted on the Village Agenda Center; confirm the current meeting schedule there.

officials.name: 222 Grace Church Street, Port Chester, NY 10573

towns.name: Town of Mount Pleasant
towns.subtitle: Central Westchester — Valhalla, Thornwood, Hawthorne
snapshot: Local law (Dec 2025) permanently bans the siting/construction of new BESS — moving from a Sept 2024–Sept 2025 moratorium to a ban. Effective immediately.
Lithium-ion fire risk; the Board cited the July 2025 US EPA BESS safety guidance and proximity to the Saw Mill River, wetlands, schools, and the Westchester Medical Center complex.
LOW — ban in place; engagement means monitoring for reconsideration or legal challenge.
summary: Mount Pleasant moved from a moratorium to a permanent ban on new BESS in December 2025. Supervisor Carl Fulgenzi said the decision followed considerable public input and study of BESS mishaps nationwide; the Board referenced the July 2025 US EPA safety guidance.
As one of the hardest-line jurisdictions in the county, Mount Pleasant is mainly a monitor-and-watch town unless the Board revisits the ban.
flags: Status shown as a permanent ban per the town's Dec 2025 prohibition local law and press release. The Carina tracker (refreshed June 26, 2026) now lists Mount Pleasant's moratorium as LIFTED (type BAN) — consistent with the moratorium having been replaced by the adopted prohibition law, which governs. Bans can be challenged or revised; track any reconsideration, variance pathway, or litigation.
committees: Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — would handle any future change.
meetings: Schedule & agendas: Town Board agendas and the meeting calendar are posted on the town site; confirm current dates there.

officials.name: Danielle Zaino (Deputy Supervisor), Mark Saracino, Joe Bonanno, Thomas Sialiano
One Town Hall Plaza, Valhalla, NY 10595

towns.name: Village/Town of Mount Kisco
towns.subtitle: Northern Westchester
snapshot: Aug 2025: Village Board voted unanimously not to classify BESS as a 'public utility,' effectively prohibiting new BESS village-wide — after twice extending a 2024 moratorium.
Mayor Cindrich said the Board wanted to protect the village until the technology improves and firefighters are better equipped for lithium-ion fires.
Mount Kisco already hosts three large-scale BESS facilities (Oakwood Cemetery/former landfill, courthouse/police roof) plus smaller units.
summary: Mount Kisco enacted a six-month moratorium in 2024 (with two three-month extensions) after a large-scale BESS was proposed near the Diamond Properties soccer field at 333 North Bedford Road. In August 2025 the Village Board effectively banned new BESS by declining to treat them as a 'public utility' under the village code.
Notably, several BESS facilities already operate in the village, so the ban applies to new development.
flags: The ban was implemented via a zoning interpretation (BESS not a 'public utility') rather than a standalone ban law; confirm the exact mechanism and any challenge. The Carina tracker (refreshed June 14, 2026) lists Mount Kisco's earlier moratorium as EXPIRED — consistent with the Village replacing the moratorium with the Aug 2025 effective ban.
committees: Village Board of Trustees — adopted the effective ban.
Planning Board & Zoning Board of Appeals — would handle any future change.
meetings: Schedule & agendas: Confirm the Village Board meeting schedule and agendas on the Mount Kisco site.
links: The Examiner — Mount Kisco BESS moratorium

towns.name: Town of Somers
towns.subtitle: Northern Westchester — rural; Lincolndale, Somers hamlet, Granite Springs
snapshot: Town amended its code to ban large-scale battery farms after a 116 MW BESS proposal (East Point Energy, Miller Road, near the Mahopac border) was withdrawn in early 2025.
Strong resident opposition (Somers and neighboring Mahopac) to a large utility-scale facility near the town line.
LOW for large-scale — code now bans big battery farms; treatment of smaller systems should be confirmed.
summary: After East Point Energy withdrew its 116-megawatt battery facility planned on Miller Road near the Somers/Mahopac border in early 2025, the Town Board amended its code to ban battery farms of that size, propelled by an active resident movement spanning Somers and Mahopac.
Somers is included as a relevant Westchester jurisdiction; confirm the exact size thresholds and whether smaller BESS remain permitted.
flags: The size threshold and the amended code section should be confirmed from the Town of Somers code/minutes; this entry is based on news reporting, not the full local law.
meetings: Confirm the Town Board meeting schedule and agendas on the Town of Somers official website.

towns.subtitle: Central Westchester — coterminous town/village government
snapshot: No BESS moratorium, ban, or dedicated BESS law identified as of the May 2026 statewide tracker.
summary: Included for completeness. Does not appear in the statewide BESS moratorium tracker (May 2026); no dedicated BESS law was identified. Not individually researched in depth — land-use authority rests with the Village Board of Trustees, Planning Board, and Board of Appeals.
flags: Reference entry — not individually researched. Confirm current BESS status, meeting schedule, and officials on the village's official site.
meetings: Confirm the Board of Trustees meeting schedule and agendas on the village's official website.

steps: Prioritize the towns mid-drafting — Cortlandt, New Castle, Pound Ridge, Bedford, Peekskill, Mamaroneck (Village), and Yonkers — where a permanent law is actively being written. Input now shapes the actual text.
Engage Greenburgh on its amendments. It already has a BESS law and is revising it via the Planning Board — an open, substantive process.
Get ahead in Rye Brook. With no moratorium yet, it is the best chance to help craft a balanced ordinance before a reactive ban.
Check Harrison and Yorktown's next moves. Both pauses lapsed; confirm whether permanent BESS codes are coming. Yorktown is also fighting state preemption (S5506/A8378).
Study Port Chester as a model. It chose permanent regulations over a ban — useful precedent and language for other towns.
Monitor the ban towns (Mount Pleasant, Mount Kisco). Bans can be revisited or challenged; watch for reconsideration or litigation.
Get on agendas in advance. Most boards require contacting the clerk/supervisor days ahead (Pound Ridge: 5 days, copy the Clerk). Public-comment periods are also available at each meeting.
Lead with the sustainability committees in each town for a “safe siting, not outright ban” message, and track the state bill S5506 / A8378.
sources: Carina Energy — NY BESS Moratoriums tracker (May 2026)
Town of Cortlandt — LL 2 of 2025 (moratorium extension)
Mamaroneck Village — Recently Adopted Local Laws 2025
Port Chester — Board of Trustees / Agenda Center
Mount Pleasant — press release: BESS ban
The Examiner — Mount Kisco BESS moratorium/ban

items: Carina Energy NY tracker refreshed June 26, 2026 reclassified three Westchester towns: Mamaroneck Village and Pound Ridge from ACTIVE to LIFTED, and Mount Pleasant (BAN) to LIFTED — dropping its Westchester active count from 8 to 5.

Verified against official town sources: Pound Ridge noticed a 2nd-extension public hearing for its BESS moratorium on March 24, 2026, and Mamaroneck Village extended its moratorium via Local Law 14-2025 (Chapter 342). Both moratoriums remain ACTIVE — statuses NOT changed; Carina's 'lifted' labels treated as aggregator lag.
Mount Pleasant kept as permanent BAN; Carina's 'lifted moratorium' is consistent with the Dec 2025 prohibition law having superseded the prior moratorium.
NY S5506 (Kavanagh) / A8378 (Levenberg) ORES standalone-storage expansion — still in the Energy & Telecommunications Committee as of June 2026; not passed.
No new Westchester municipality adopted a BESS rule; no new public hearing/vote surfaced for the other monitored towns (Cortlandt, New Castle, Bedford, Peekskill, Yonkers).
Re-confirmed every town's status against the Carina Energy NY tracker, which was refreshed June 14, 2026 (now 104 active NY moratoriums statewide; Westchester tied for the most with 8).

Tracker cross-check for the 8 Westchester active-moratorium towns held: Cortlandt, New Castle, Pound Ridge (ALL_BESS), Bedford, Peekskill, Mamaroneck (Village), Yonkers — all ACTIVE; Harrison and Yorktown remain EXPIRED; Port Chester remains off the moratorium list (regulated).

Noted tracker nuances: the moratorium tracker now lists Mount Pleasant as ACTIVE and Mount Kisco as EXPIRED, while this dashboard reports each as an effective BAN based on their own town actions (Mount Pleasant's Dec 2025 prohibition law; Mount Kisco's Aug 2025 'not a public utility' interpretation). Statuses kept as bans; see flags.
NY S5506 (Kavanagh) / A8378 (Levenberg) — ORES standalone-storage expansion — still in the Energy & Telecommunications Committee; not passed either chamber.
No new Westchester municipality appeared on the tracker; no new public BESS hearing/vote surfaced for any monitored town since the prior run.
Baseline dashboard build prior to daily-refresh changelog tracking.
